Allows servers to change the properties of entities, beyond changing their position and yaw/pitch.

'''Motivation''': Allow for greater customisation, while also allowing for extension to more properties in the future.

'''Client Behavior''': Client must be able to receive '''''SetEntityProperty''''' packets after the '''''[[Minecraft Wiki:Projects/wiki.vg merge/Classic_Protocol#Server_.E2.86.92_Client_packets|LevelFinalize]]''''' packet for the current level has been received from the server.

'''Server Behavior''': Server may send '''''SetEntityProperty''''' packets to the client after the '''''[[Minecraft Wiki:Projects/wiki.vg merge/Classic_Protocol#Server_.E2.86.92_Client_packets|LevelFinalize]]''''' packet for the current level has been sent to the client.

<h4>SetEntityProperty packet</h4>
''Server to Client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="3" | 0x2A
(42)
| class="col1 centeralign" | EntityID
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Between 0 and 127
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Property type
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4" | Enumeration of properties that can be changed
*0 = Model X rotation in degrees
*1 = Model Y (yaw) rotation in degrees
*2 = Model Z rotation in degrees
|- class="row3"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Property value
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | 45
| class="col4 centeralign" | New value for the given property.
|- class="row12"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 7 bytes
|}

===ExtEntityPositions===
Expands entity positions from the range of [-1024, 1023] to [-67,108,864, 67,108,863].

'''Motivation''': Allows for players to interact and see other players properly on very large/tall/long worlds.

'''Client Behavior''': Client must read 32 instead of 16 bit integers for the '''''AddEntity''''', '''''EntityTeleport''''', '''''ExtAddEntity2''''', '''''ExtEntityTeleport''''', and '''''SetSpawnpoint''''' packets. It must write 32 instead of 16 bit integers for the '''''Position and Orientation''''' packet sent to the server.

'''Server Behavior''': Server must write 32 instead of 16 bit integers for the '''''AddEntity''''', '''''EntityTeleport''''', '''''ExtAddEntity2''''', '''''ExtEntityTeleport''''', and '''''SetSpawnpoint''''' packets. It must read 32 instead of 16 bit integers for the '''''Position and Orientation''''' packet received from the client.

'''Note:''' No new packets are defined by this extension, instead, the reading and writing behaviour of existing packets is modified. The behaviour change only applies to the X, Y, Z fields of the aforementioned packets.

===TwoWayPing===
Allows servers and clients to send identifiable ping packets.

'''Motivation''': Allows clients and servers to calculate ping statistics.

'''Client Behavior''': Periodic '''''TwoWayPing''''' packets should be sent to the server, with a ''Direction'' of ''0''. Behavior upon receipt of a '''''TwoWayPing''''' packet depends on ''Direction'':

*If 0, update ping statistics. (Typically using difference between current time and time a ping packet with a matching ''Data'' was sent)
*Else, immediately send the packet back to the server.
'''Server Behavior''': Periodic '''''TwoWayPing''''' packets should be sent to the client, with a ''Direction'' of ''1''. Behavior upon receipt of a '''''TwoWayPing''''' packet depends on ''Direction'':

*If 0, immediately send the packet back to the client.
*Else update ping statistics. (Typically using difference between current time and time a ping packet with a matching ''Data'' was sent)

<h4>TwoWayPing packet</h4>

''Bidirectional''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="2" | 0x2B
(43)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Direction
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Direction of the ping.
*0 if packet is client to server ping
*1 if packet is server to client ping
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Data
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>4534</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Unique/Random data for this particular ping packet.
Data is usually just from a counter that is incremented each time this packet is sent.
|- class="row12"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 4 bytes
|}

===InventoryOrder===
Allows servers to explicitly control the inventory's layout.

'''Motivation''': Allows users greater over the inventory, such as grouping related blocks, hiding blocks, etc.

'''Client Behavior''': If ''Order'' is 0, client must remove the given block from the inventory. Otherwise, the given inventory slot must be set to the given block (''e.g. Stone by default has slot 1''). Clients must not perform any reordering or eliding of slots. As such, there may be gaps between inventory slots, <del>or the same block put in multiple inventory slots</del>. (See the note below.) Clients may optionally choose to not render completely empty rows in the inventory.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers decide how to handle multiple blocks being assigned to the same inventory slot, before sending '''SetInventoryOrder''' packets to the client. For example, servers may decide to do nothing, in which case the client shows the inventory slot as the block from the last packet sent. Alternatively, server may decide to move conflicting blocks into other free inventory slots, or some other method.

Note: The SetInventoryOrder packet was previously documented here with the fields reversed, but implementations in practice specify the BlockId field first. Additionally, ClassiCube as of version 1.3.8 does not allow for the same block in multiple slots.<ref>[https://github.com/ClassiCube/ClassiCube/blob/b9099d4216229c55bc5ad82dcebd0e565dff5160/src/Protocol.c#L1453 ClassiCube src/Protocol.c, line 1453]</ref>


<h4>SetInventoryOrder packet</h4>

''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="2" | 0x2C
(44)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Block ID
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>45</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | ID of the block being removed from or put in the inventory.
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Order
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Order of the block.
*Order of 0 completely hides the block from inventory
*Else, order is the 1-based position to put the block in
|- class="row12"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 3 bytes
|}

===InstantMOTD===
Indicates a client supports receiving '''''Server Identification''''' packets at any time, not just before a map is sent.

'''Motivation''': Sending '''''Server Identification''''' packets is useful for changing MOTD variables (such as hacks statuses). However, some clients behave unpredictably when receiving a '''''Server Identification''''' packet without the whole map being resent afterwards.

'''Client Behavior''': Clients must be prepared to receive '''''Server Identification''''' packets at any time. Do not assume that a new map will be sent after receiving this packet.

'''Server Behavior''': Server must keep track of which clients it can safely send only '''''Server Identification''''' packets to, and which clients require the whole map to be resent afterwards.

'''Note:''' No new packets are defined by this extension. 

===ExtendedBlocks===
Allows using more than 256 unique block types.

'''Motivation''': Allows for more detailed maps by allowing up to 768 instead of 256 different block types in a map.

'''Client Behavior''': Clients must adjust how they read the map as described in the '''Affect on map sending''' section. Clients must also adjust how they read and write several fields of the packets described in the '''Affect on other packets''' section. When a client detects that the secondary map blocks array is used, it must switch from accessing blocks via <code>map.blocks[index]</code> to <code>map1.blocks[index] | (map2.blocks[index] << 8)</code> instead.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers must adjust how they write the map as described in the '''Affect on map sending''' section. Servers must also adjust how they read and write several fields of the packets described in the '''Affect on other packets''' section. Ideally, servers should avoid sending a secondary map blocks array when possible to minimise client memory usage.


<h4>Affect on map sending</h4>

Essentially, instead of '''LevelDataChunk''' packets just being used to send the compressed form of the map blocks array, it is repurposed to send both the compressed form of the 'main' map blocks array and optionally a secondary map blocks array.
To accomplish this, the '''Percent Complete''' field in the '''LevelDataChunk''' packet is repurposed. A value of ''0'' indicates that this LevelDataChunk packet contains part of the compressed data for the 'main' map blocks array, while a value of ''1'' indicates this LevelDataChunk packet contains part of the compressed data for the optional secondary map blocks array.

The client and server should treat the '''LevelDataChunk''' packets used for the 'main' map blocks array as completely separate from the secondary map blocks array, i.e. the 'main' map blocks array should be compressed and decompressed completely independently of the optional secondary map blocks array.

'''Note:''' Even if the server only sends a 'main' map blocks array, the client must still be prepared to allocate a secondary map blocks array at any time after map load. For example:
* Client A goes to map XYZ
* Server sends client A just a main blocks array for map XYZ
* A minute later, client B places a block with ID 510 on map XYZ
* Server then broadcasts this block change to all clients on map XYZ
Since a secondary map blocks array was not sent during map load by the server, Client A must now allocate a secondary map blocks array. 

<h4>Affect on other packets</h4>

Several fields in some packets are expanded in size. These are:
{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | New Type
! class="col3" | Direction
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" | Set Block (0x05)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Block type
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Client -> Server
|- class="row2"
| class="col0 centeralign" | Set Block (0x06)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Block Type
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Server -> Client
|- class="row3"
| class="col0 centeralign" | Position and Orientation (0x08)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Player ID
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Client -> Server
|- class="row4"
| class="col0 centeralign" | HoldThis (0x14)
| class="col1 centeralign" | BlockToHold
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Server -> Client
|- class="row5"
| class="col0 centeralign" | SetBlockPermission (0x1C)
| class="col1 centeralign" | BlockType
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Server -> Client
|- class="row6"
| class="col0 centeralign" | DefineBlock (0x23)
| class="col1 centeralign" | BlockID
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Server -> Client
|- class="row7"
| class="col0 centeralign" | UndefineBlock (0x24)
| class="col1 centeralign" | BlockID
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Server -> Client
|- class="row8"
| class="col0 centeralign" | DefineBlockExt (0x25)
| class="col1 centeralign" | BlockID
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Server -> Client
|- class="row9"
| class="col0 centeralign" | BulkBlockUpdate (0x28)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Blocks
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte[320]
| class="col3 centeralign" | Server -> Client
|- class="row10"
| class="col0 centeralign" | SetInventoryOrder (0x2C)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Order
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Server -> Client
|- class="row11"
| class="col0 centeralign" | SetInventoryOrder (0x2C)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Block ID
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Server -> Client
|- class="row12"
| class="col0 centeralign" | SetHotbar (0x2D)
| class="col1 centeralign" | BlockID
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | Server -> Client
|}

<h5>Affect on BulkBlockUpdate</h5>

Although the affect on most packets is straightforward, the changes to '''BulkBlockUpdate''' requires a more thorough explanation:
* The first 256 bytes store the lower 8 bits of the ID for each of the 256 blocks. 
* The remaining 64 bytes store the higher 2 bits of the ID for each of the 256 blocks, with 4 of these packed into 1 byte.

Code example for how a client would read the block ids from '''BulkBlockUpdate''' when '''ExtendedBlocks''' is mutually supported:
<syntaxhighlight lang="Pascal">
var block_ids = new short[256];
for (var i = 0; i < count; i++)
    block_ids[i] = Blocks[i]
end for

for (var i = 0; i < count; i += 4) {
    var flags = Blocks[256 + (i >> 2)]
    block_ids[i + 0] |= ((flags & 0x03) >> 0) << 8
    block_ids[i + 1] |= ((flags & 0x0C) >> 2) << 8
    block_ids[i + 2] |= ((flags & 0x30) >> 4) << 8
    block_ids[i + 3] |= ((flags & 0xC0) >> 6) << 8
end for
</syntaxhighlight>

===FastMap===
Reduces load on clients and servers by reducing the complexity of sending the map.

'''Motivation''': Clients and servers no longer need to compute or validate a CRC32 for the map data. Clients can also pre-allocate output buffers for decompression.

'''Client Behavior''': '''''LevelInit''''' packet data is changed to consist of a 32 bit integer (instead of no data), representing the size of the map. Data in '''''LevelDataChunk''''' packets must be decompressed using DEFLATE instead of GZIP algorithm.

'''Server Behavior''': Server must write a 32 bit integer for the '''''LevelInit''''' packet data, representing the size of the map. Data in '''''LevelDataChunk''''' packets must be compressed using DEFLATE instead of GZIP algorithm.

'''Note:''' The uncompressed data is no longer prefixed by the size of the map, it purely consists of the data of the map.

'''Note:''' DEFLATE means raw deflate, no ZLib header or footer. Behaviour of clients when decompressed data size does not equal the size specified in '''''LevelInit''''' packet is unspecified.

===ExtendedTextures===
Allows using more than 256 unique textures.

'''Motivation''': Allows for more detailed maps, since each block can use up to six different textures.

'''Client Behavior''': Clients must read unsigned 16 bit integers (instead of 8 bit) for the ''TopTextureID,SideTextureID,BottomTextureID'' fields in a  '''''DefineBlock''''' packet, and the ''TopTextureID,LeftTextureId,RightTextureId,FrontTextureId,BackTextureId,BottomTextureID'' fields in a '''''DefineBlockExt''''' packet. The texture ID should be read modulo client's maximum number of supported textures. (i.e. If client supports up to 512 textures, use ''ReadU16() & 0x1FF'')

'''Server Behavior''': Servers must write unsigned 16 bit integers (instead of 8 bit) for the aforementioned fields in '''''DefineBlock''''' and  '''''DefineBlockExt''''' packets. Texture IDs should be written as 8 bits modulo 256 (i.e. ''WriteU8(id & 0xFF)'') for non-supporting clients.


'''Note:''' Displaying more textures is achieved by increasing ''terrain.png'' to be 256x512 instead of 256x256 in size (or a multiple of that). Hence, [[Minecraft Wiki:Projects/wiki.vg merge/Classic_Protocol_Extension#EnvMapAppearance|EnvMapAppearance extension]] or [[Minecraft Wiki:Projects/wiki.vg merge/Classic_Protocol_Extension#EnvMapAspect|EnvMapAspect extension]] must be mutually supported for this extension to be useful.

===SetHotbar===
Allows servers to set the contents of a player's hotbar.

'''Motivation''': Gives some control to the server over what the player holds.

'''Client Behavior''': Clients must set the block at the given hotbar position upon receiving the '''SetHotbar''' packet.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers may send the '''SetHotbar''' packet to change the block at a specific position in a player's hotbar.


<h4>SetHotbar packet</h4>

''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="2" | 0x2D
(45)
| class="col1 centeralign" | BlockID
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>1</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The ID of the block that will occupy the hotbar position.
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | HotbarIndex
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The position number of the hotbar, from 0 to 8. A value of 0 represents the leftmost position.
|- class="row3"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 3 bytes
|}

===SetSpawnpoint===
Allows servers to directly set the spawn position and orientation of players.

'''Motivation''': Allows setting the spawn of a player without changing their current position. This is to overcome the problem where sending a '''AddEntity''' packet with an '''EntityID''' of ''255'' to update's a client spawn also causes them to be repositioned back several blocks if they are currently moving due to latency, which is noticeably jarring and annoying to users.

'''Client Behavior''': Clients must update their spawn using the exact same logic that they use to update their spawn upon receiving a '''AddEntity''' or '''ExtAddEntity2''' packet with an '''EntityID''' of ''255''.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers may send the '''SetSpawnpoint''' packet to anytime to update the client's spawn. (Note that clients will still continue to update their spawn upon receiving a '''AddEntity''' or '''ExtAddEntity2''' packet with an '''EntityID''' of ''255'')


<h5>SetSpawnpoint packet</h5>

''Server to Client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="5" | 0x2E
(46)
| class="col1 centeralign" | SpawnX
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>1</code>
| class="col4" | X coordinate (32 units per block) of entity's spawn location.
|- class="row5"
| class="col1 centeralign" | SpawnY
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>2</code>
| class="col4" | Y coordinate (32 units per block) of entity's spawn location.
|- class="row6"
| class="col1 centeralign" | SpawnZ
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>3</code>
| class="col4" | Z coordinate (32 units per block) of entity's spawn location.
|- class="row7"
| class="col1 centeralign" | SpawnYaw
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>4</code>
| class="col4" | Orientation (left-right) at the entity's spawn location.
|- class="row8"
| class="col1 centeralign" | SpawnPitch
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>5</code>
| class="col4" | Orientation (up-down) at the entity's spawn location.
|- class="row9"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 9 bytes
|}

===VelocityControl===
Allows servers to affect the velocity of players.

'''Motivation''': Allows the server to affect the velocity of a client to make that client jump, knockback that client, etc.

'''Client Behavior''': Clients must adjust their X, Y, and Z velocity based on the fields read from the '''VelocityControl''' packet.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers may send the '''VelocityControl''' packet at anytime (except during map load) to update the client's velocity.


<h5>VelocityControl packet</h5>

''Server to Client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="6" | 0x2F
(47)
| class="col1 centeralign" | VelocityX
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>1000</code>
| class="col4" | X velocity, scaled such that a value of 10000 corresponds to a velocity of 1.0.
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | VelocityY
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>12330</code>
| class="col4" | Y velocity, scaled such that a value of 10000 corresponds to a velocity of 1.0.
|- class="row3"
| class="col1 centeralign" | VelocityZ
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>3000</code>
| class="col4" | Z velocity, scaled such that a value of 10000 corresponds to a velocity of 1.0.
|- class="row4"
| class="col1 centeralign" | ModeX
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4" | 
*0 = ''VelocityX'' is added to Client's X velocity.
*1 = Client's X velocity is set to ''VelocityX''.
|- class="row5"
| class="col1 centeralign" | ModeY
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>1</code>
| class="col4" |
*0 = ''VelocityY'' is added to Client's Y velocity.
*1 = Client's Y velocity is set to ''VelocityY''.
|- class="row6"
| class="col1 centeralign" | ModeZ
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4" | 
*0 = ''VelocityZ'' is added to Client's Z velocity.
*1 = Client's Z velocity is set to ''VelocityZ''.
|- class="row7"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 16 bytes
|}

As an example, to make the client jump up 1.233 blocks without affecting horizontal velocity, send a VelocityControl packet with:
''VelocityX'' = 0, ''VelocityY'' = 12330, ''VelocityZ'' = 0, ''ModeX'' = 0, ''ModeY'' = 1, ''ModeZ'' = 0

===CustomParticles===
Allows servers to define and display particle effects in the game.

'''Motivation''': The ability to show custom particles can be used to visually enhance map environments, such as making leaves fall from trees or making water splash.

'''Client Behavior''': Clients must be able to receive the packets defined by this extention at any time after level loading. Clients must render particles with the proper attributes defined by the '''DefineEffect''' packet upon reception of a '''SpawnEffect''' packet. Clients should ignore '''SpawnEffect''' packets with an ''EffectID'' that is not associated with a defined particle.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers may send any of these packets at any time after level loading. Servers should only send '''SpawnEffect''' packets with an ''EffectID'' associated with an effect defined by the '''DefineEffect''' packet.


<h4>DefineEffect packet</h4>

''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="19" | 0x30
(48)
| class="col1 centeralign" | EffectID
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The ID used to identify this effect for the '''SpawnEffect''' packet.
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | U1
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | U-coordinate of the first UV coordinate pair used to define the particle texture rectangle.
Assuming particles.png is 256x256 pixels, these UV coordinates correspond to the actual pixel coordinates.
|- class="row3"
| class="col1 centeralign" | V1
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | V-coordinate of the first UV coordinate pair used to define the particle texture rectangle.
Assuming particles.png is 256x256 pixels, these UV coordinates correspond to the actual pixel coordinates.
|- class="row4"
| class="col1 centeralign" | U2
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>7</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | U-coordinate of the second UV coordinate pair used to define the particle texture rectangle.
Assuming particles.png is 256x256 pixels, these UV coordinates correspond to the actual pixel coordinates.
|- class="row5"
| class="col1 centeralign" | V2
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>7</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | V-coordinate of the second UV coordinate pair used to define the particle texture rectangle.
Assuming particles.png is 256x256 pixels, these UV coordinates correspond to the actual pixel coordinates.
|- class="row6"
| class="col1 centeralign" | RedTint
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>255</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Red tint applied to the particle's texture.
|- class="row7"
| class="col1 centeralign" | GreenTint
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>255</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Green tint applied to the particle's texture.
|- class="row8"
| class="col1 centeralign" | BlueTint
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>255</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Blue tint applied to the particle's texture.
|- class="row9"
| class="col1 centeralign" | FrameCount
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>4</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The number of animation frames used for the particles in this effect.
The frames are always stored sequentially left to right in particles.png.
|- class="row10"
| class="col1 centeralign" | ParticleCount
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>7</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The number of particles spawned by the reception of a '''SpawnEffect''' referring to this effect.
|- class="row11"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Size
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>16</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The size of the particle in fixed-point player position units.
|- class="row12"
| class="col1 centeralign" | SizeVariation
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>100</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The amount of variation possible in the size of each particle.
This value is scaled such that a value of <code>10000</code> corresponds to a value of <code>100%</code>. Cannot be negative.
|- class="row13"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Spread
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>3</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The maximum distance in fixed-point player position units from the spawn point at which a particle may spawn.
|- class="row14"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Speed
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>50</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The speed at which the particles will move away from the origin.
|- class="row15"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Gravity
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>7</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The amount subtracted from the particles' y-velocity each second.
This value is scaled such that a value of <code>10000</code> corresponds to a value of <code>1</code>.
|- class="row16"
| class="col1 centeralign" | BaseLifetime
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>20000</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The average amount of time in seconds that the particles' stay visible if not colliding.
This value is scaled such that a value of <code>10000</code> corresponds to a value of <code>1</code>. Cannot be negative.
|- class="row17"
| class="col1 centeralign" | LifetimeVariation
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>5000</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The amount of percentage variation from the BaseLifetime in the particles' lifetimes.
This value is scaled such that a value of <code>10000</code> corresponds to a value of <code>100%</code>. Cannot be negative.
|- class="row18"
| class="col1 centeralign" | CollideFlags
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0x60</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | This is a bitfield that affects the behavior of the particles upon collision with a block.
Bit 7: Expiration policy
*0 = Expire upon collision with collidable walls or ceilings, but stop moving upon collision with collidable floors.
*1 = Expire upon collision with collidable walls, ceilings, or floors.
Bit 6: Collision with blocks with solid, ice, or slippery ice collision properties.
*0 = Particles do not collide with blocks with one of these collision properties.
*1 = Particles will collide with blocks with one of these collision properties.
Bit 5: Collision with blocks with water-like, lava-like, or rope-like collision properties.
*0 = Particles do not collide with blocks with one of these collision properties.
*1 = Particles will collide with blocks with one of these collision properties.
Bit 4: Collision with blocks with leaf-like draw properties.
*0 = Particles do not collide with blocks with this draw property, regardless of their collision property.
*1 = Particles will collide with blocks with this draw property.
|- class="row19"
| class="col1 centeralign" | FullBright
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" |
*0 = Particle is lit normally.
*1 = Particle renders with its texture unaffected by lighting.
|- class="row20"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 36 bytes
|}

<h4>SpawnEffect packet</h4>

''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="7" | 0x31
(49)
| class="col1 centeralign" | EffectID
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The ID of the effect to be displayed.
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | PositionX
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>4000</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The x-coordinate of the position in player position units at which the effect appears.
|- class="row3"
| class="col1 centeralign" | PositionY
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>1280</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The y-coordinate of the position in player position units at which the effect appears.
|- class="row4"
| class="col1 centeralign" | PositionZ
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>4000</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The z-coordinate of the position in player position units at which the effect appears.
|- class="row5"
| class="col1 centeralign" | OriginX
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>4000</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The x-coordinate of the position in player position units from which the particles move away.
|- class="row6"
| class="col1 centeralign" | OriginY
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>1280</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The y-coordinate of the position in player position units from which the particles move away.
|- class="row7"
| class="col1 centeralign" | OriginZ
| class="col2 centeralign" | int
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>4000</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The z-coordinate of the position in player position units from which the particles move away.
|- class="row8"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 26 bytes
|}

===CustomModels v2===
Allows servers to define custom entity models.

'''Motivation''': The ability to show custom models can be used to visually enhance entities.

'''Client Behavior''': Clients must be able to receive the packets defined by this extension at any time. Clients should ignore '''DefineModelPart''' and '''UndefineModel''' packets with an ''ModelID'' that is not associated with a defined model.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers may send any of these packets at any time. Servers should only send '''DefineModelPart''' packets with an ''ModelID'' associated with a custom model defined by the '''DefineModel''' packet.


<h4>DefineModel packet</h4>

''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="11" | 0x32
(50)
| class="col1 centeralign" | ModelID
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The ID of the model to be defined or replaced.
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Name
| class="col2 centeralign" | string
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>TestModel</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The name of the model (Can be used with [[#ChangeModel|'''ChangeModel''']] packet)
|- class="row3"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Flags
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Flags for this entity model.
* 0x01 = bobbing (whether bobbing should be applied to entities using this model when rendering)
* 0x02 = pushes (whether an entity using this model pushes other entities it is touching)
* 0x04 = uses human skin (whether to still treat non-url skins as skins)
* 0x08 = calc human anims (whether to use humanoid arm animations)
|- class="row4"
| class="col1 centeralign" | NameY
| class="col2 centeralign" | float
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>1.2</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | How far above the entity's origin that the name texture should appear at.
|- class="row5"
| class="col1 centeralign" | EyeY
| class="col2 centeralign" | float
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0.7</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | How far above the entity's origin that the camera should be positioned at.
|- class="row6"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Collision size
| class="col2 centeralign" | Vector3
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(1.2,0.8,1.2)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Size of the collision box around the entity
|- class="row7"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Picking bounds min
| class="col2 centeralign" | Vector3
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(-0.4,0,-0.4)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Minimum corner of the AABB around the entity for clicking detection ([[#PlayerClick|'''PlayerClick''']])
|- class="row8"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Picking bounds max
| class="col2 centeralign" | Vector3
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(0.4,0.8,0.4)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Maximum corner of the AABB around the entity for clicking detection ([[#PlayerClick|'''PlayerClick''']])
|- class="row9"
| class="col1 centeralign" | U scale
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>32</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Scaling factor applied to horizontal texture coordinates.
|- class="row10"
| class="col1 centeralign" | V scale
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>64</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Scaling factor applied to vertical texture coordinates.
|- class="row11"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Parts count
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>16</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The number of parts/boxes that constitute this model.
|- class="row13"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 116 bytes
|}

<h4>DefineModelPart packet</h4>

''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="16" | 0x33
(51)
| class="col1 centeralign" | ModelID
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The ID of the model to be updated.
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Minimum coords
| class="col2 centeralign" | Vector3
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(0.1,0.1,0.1)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The minimum corner coordinate of this box.
|- class="row3"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Maximum coords
| class="col2 centeralign" | Vector3
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(0.9,0.9,0.9)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The maximum corner coordinate of this box.
|- class="row4"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Top face UV
| class="col2 centeralign" | UVCoords
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(0,0, 8,8)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The UV coordinates for the four coordinates of the top face of this box.
|- class="row5"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Bottom face UV
| class="col2 centeralign" | UVCoords
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(8,8, 16,16)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The UV coordinates for the four coordinates of the top face of this box.
|- class="row6"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Front face UV
| class="col2 centeralign" | UVCoords
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(16,16, 24,32)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The UV coordinates for the four coordinates of the front face of this box.
|- class="row7"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Back face UV
| class="col2 centeralign" | UVCoords
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(24,16, 32,32)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The UV coordinates for the four coordinates of the back face of this box.
|- class="row8"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Left face UV
| class="col2 centeralign" | UVCoords
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(32,16, 40,32)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The UV coordinates for the four coordinates of the left face of this box.
|- class="row9"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Right face UV
| class="col2 centeralign" | UVCoords
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(40,16, 48,32)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The UV coordinates for the four coordinates of the right face of this box.
|- class="row10"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Rotation origin
| class="col2 centeralign" | Vector3
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(0.5,0.9,0.5)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The coordinate around which this box is rotated
|- class="row11"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Rotation angles
| class="col2 centeralign" | Vector3
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(45,0,0)</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The base rotation angles in degrees.
|- class="row12"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Animation 1
| class="col2 centeralign" | AnimData
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>Type=Spin,Axis=X,a=5,b=2</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | First animation to apply to this box.
|- class="row13"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Animation 2
| class="col2 centeralign" | AnimData
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>Type=None</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Second animation to apply to this box.
|- class="row14"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Animation 3
| class="col2 centeralign" | AnimData
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>Type=None</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Third animation to apply to this box.
|- class="row15"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Animation 4
| class="col2 centeralign" | AnimData
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>Type=None</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Fourth animation to apply to this box.
|- class="row16"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Flags
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Flags to apply to this box.
* 0x01 = full bright (whether this box should always be rendered at full brightness)
* 0x02 = first person arm (whether this box should be shown when holding air, like how arm of humanoid model is)
|- class="row17"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 167 bytes
|}

<h4>UndefineModel packet</h4>

''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="1" | 0x34
(52)
| class="col1 centeralign" | ModelID
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The ID of the custom model to be removed.
|- class="row2"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 2 bytes
|}

<h4>Data structures</h4>

{| class="wikitable"
|+ <code>Vector3</code>
! Field Name
! Field Type
! Meaning
|-
| X
| float
| X coordinate
|-
| Y
| float
| Y coordinate
|-
| Z
| float
| Z coordinate
|}
{| class="wikitable"
|+ <code>UVCoords</code>
! Field Name
! Field Type
! Meaning
|-
| U1
| Unsigned short
| First horizontal texture coordinate
|-
| V1
| Unsigned short
| First vertical texture coordinate
|-
| U2
| Unsigned short
| Second horizontal texture coordinate
|-
| V2
| Unsigned short
| Second vertical texture coordinate
|}
{| class="wikitable"
|+ <code>AnimData</code>
! Field Name
! Field Type
! Meaning
|-
| Flags
| byte
| The upper 2 bits specify which axis this animation applies to.
* 0 = X axis
* 1 = Y axis
* 2 = Z axis
The lower 6 bits specify the type of this animation.
* 0 = None
* 1 = Head
* 2 = LeftLegX
* 3 = RightLegX
* 4 = LeftArmX
* 5 = LeftArmZ
* 6 = RightArmX
* 7 = RightArmZ
* 8 = Spin
* 9 = SpinVelocity
* 10 = SinRotate
* 11 = SinRotateVelocity
* 12 = SinTranslate
* 13 = SinTranslateVelocity
* 14 = SinSize
* 15 = SinSizeVelocity
* 16 = FlipRotate
* 17 = FlipRotateVelocity
* 18 = FlipTranslate
* 19 = FlipTranslateVelocity
* 20 = FlipSize
* 21 = FlipSizeVelocity
|-
| a
| float
| First animation argument
|-
| b
| float
| Second animation argument
|-
| c
| float
| Third animation argument
|-
| d
| float
| Fourth animation argument
|}

===PluginMessage===
Allows server plugins and client plugins to communicate with each other.

'''Motivation''': To enable custom client-side and server-side plugins to exchange information.

'''Client Behavior''': When the client receives this packet, it should trigger an event for its plugins, passing along the channel and the string message. Clients can also send this packet when a plugin needs to send a message to the server.

'''Server Behavior''': When the server receives this packet, it should trigger an event for its plugins, passing along the channel and the string message. Servers can also send this packet when a plugin needs to send a message to the client.


<h4>PluginMessage packet</h4>

''Bidirectional''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="2" | 0x35<br>(53)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Channel
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>1</code>
| class="col4" | Identifies the "channel" of the message
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Message
| class="col2 centeralign" | string
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>"Some plugin message"</code>
| class="col4" | The string message itself
|- class="row3"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 66 bytes
|}

===ExtEntityTeleport===
Allows server greater control over how the client teleports entities.

'''Motivation''': Increased possibilities when teleporting as well as improved visual result.

'''Client Behavior''': Client must be able to receive this packet any time it can receive a Player Teleport packet (0x08) and teleport as specified by the packet.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers may send the '''ExtEntityTeleport''' packet to teleport entities.


<h4>ExtEntityTeleport packet</h4>

''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="7" | 0x36
(54)
| class="col1 centeralign" | EntityID
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>127</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Same behavior as packet 0x08 (Player teleport)
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Teleport behavior
| class="col2 centeralign" | TeleportBehavior
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>(binary) 00110101</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | How the teleport should be performed. See below for TeleportBehavior data structure
|- class="row3"
| class="col1 centeralign" | X
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>16</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | X value (32 units per block) for the teleport
|- class="row4"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Y
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>51</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Y value (32 units per block) for the teleport
|- class="row5"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Z
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>16</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Z value (32 units per block) for the teleport
|- class="row6"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Yaw (heading)
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>63</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Orientation value (left-right) for the teleport
|- class="row7"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Pitch
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Orientation value (up-down) for the teleport
|- class="row8"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 11 bytes
|}

<h4>Data structure</h4>

{| class="wikitable"
|+ <code>TeleportBehavior</code>
! Field Name
! Bits occupied
! Field type
! Meaning
|-
| Use position
| 1
| boolean
| Does this teleport affect position?
|-
| Move mode
| 2-3
| enum
|
* 0 = Move position to X Y Z instantly with no interpolation
* 1 = Move position to X Y Z smoothly with interpolation
* 2 = Move to current position + X Y Z smoothly with interpolation
* 3 = Move to current position + X Y Z seamlessly with no interpolation
|-
| Unused
| 4
|
|
|-
| Use orientation
| 5
| boolean
| Does this teleport affect orientation (yaw and pitch)?
|-
| Interpolate orientation
| 6
| boolean
| Does orientation interpolate smoothly?
|-
| Unused
| 7-8
|
|
|}
Unsigned byte holding four fields within its 8 bits.

1 = least significant bit (rightmost)

8 = most significant bit (leftmost)


===LightingMode===
Indicates support for various ways to light maps clientside and allows server greater control over which lighting mode should be used.

'''Motivation''': Improved visual results, especially in dark levels. Allowing map creators to ensure their level is lit in whatever way looks best.

'''Client Behavior''': Client must be able to receive this packet any time and switch to the lighting mode specified.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers may send the '''LightingMode''' packet to change a client's lighting mode.


<h4>LightingMode packet</h4>

''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="2" | 0x37
(55)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Lighting Mode
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>2</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Enumeration of lighting modes
* 0 = Revert lighting mode to client's local setting
* 1 = Classic
* 2 = Fancy
Unsupported values should simply be ignored.
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Locked
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>1</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Should the client be prevented from changing their lighting mode?
This setting is ignored when reverting to user's local setting with mode 0.
|- class="row3"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 3 bytes
|}


'''Classic lighting mode'''
* The world is lit just like Minecraft Classic.
* There is a sun color and a shadow color and blocks can cast shadows directly downward.

'''Fancy lighting mode'''
* Sunlight and shadow act identically to Classic lighting mode.
* Bright blocks are able to cast a much wider (15 blocks in both directions at most) range of light.
* Bright blocks are able to cast 1 of 2 new environment colors: LavaLight and LampLight.
* With EnvColors extension, the server may send LavaLight and LampLight colors using the enumeration values of 6 and 7 respectively.
* LavaLight should use a default color of #FFEBC6 and LampLight should use a default color of #FFFFFF
* By default, the client should give bright blocks such as lava a LavaLight strength of 15.

'''Fancy lighting mode interacts with the "BlockDefinitions" extension and changes how the <code>FullBright</code> field from DefineBlock packet should be read'''
* If FullBright is zero, the block should be treated as usual.
* Else if the leftmost (most significant) bit of FullBright is 0, and the overall value is non-zero, the block should be treated as casting LavaLight strength of 15.
* Else if the leftmost (most significant) bit of FullBright is 1, then the FullBright value should be treated as a LightInfo Structure.
* If the "BlockDefinitionsExt" extension is enabled, this also applies to the <code>FullBright</code> field in the DefineBlockExt packet.

<h4>LightInfo structure</h4>

{| class="wikitable"
|+ <code>LightInfo</code>
! Field Name
! Bits occupied
! Field type
! Meaning
|-
| Brightness
| 1-4
| unsigned integer (0-15)
| How far this block should cast light.
|-
| Unused
| 5-6
|
|
|-
| IsLampLightColor
| 7
| boolean
| Should this block cast the LampLight color? If no, use LavaLight color
|-
| IsBrightnessStructure
| 8
| boolean
| Flag to determine if the FullBright field should be treated as this structure.
|}
Unsigned byte holding three fields within its 8 bits.

1 = least significant bit (rightmost)

8 = most significant bit (leftmost)


===CinematicGui===
Changes properties of the player's gui to achieve cinematic effects.

'''Motivation''': Allows servers to add black bars to signify to the player that they have initiated a cinematic. Can also be used for things like drowning/health indicators by setting aperture size to full and decreasing the opacity.

'''Client Behavior''': Must hide hand/hotbar/crosshair if those are specified to be disabled. Must render cinematic bars over the game with the supplied aperture/RGB/opacity values.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers may control whether hand/hotbar/crosshair is supposed to be hidden. Server may also configure the appearance of the cinematic bars.


<h4>CinematicGui packet</h4>

''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="8" | 0x38
(56)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Hide crosshair
| class="col2 centeralign" | boolean
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0/1</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Toggles the visibility of the crosshair.
*0 = show
*1 = hide
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Hide hand
| class="col2 centeralign" | boolean
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0/1</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Toggles the visibility of the player's hand.
*0 = show
*1 = hide
|- class="row3"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Hide hotbar
| class="col2 centeralign" | boolean
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0/1</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Toggles the visibility of the hotbar.
*0 = show
*1 = hide
|- class="row4"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Red
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>255</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Red component of the cinematic bars.
|- class="row5"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Green
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>0</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Green component of the cinematic bars.
|- class="row6"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Blue
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>127</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Blue component of the cinematic bars.
|- class="row7"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Opacity
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>255</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Alpha component of the cinematic bars.
*0 = not visible
*127 = half visible
*255 = visible
|- class="row8"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Aperture size
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>9830</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Aperture size works like widescreen or cutscene bars. You can use 65535 to completely cover the screen. Designed to be (0-1 * 65535), where 1 = fully covered, 0.5 = half cover, and 0 = no cover.
|- class="row12"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 10 bytes
|}

===NotifyAction===
Allows servers to detect when useful events happen in the client.

'''Motivation''': Primary motivations consist of detecting level saves or respawns/setspawns.

'''Client Behavior''': Clients must send a packet when certain events happen, along with any data relevant to the event.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers may listen for NotifyAction packets.


<h4>NotifyAction packet</h4>

''Client to server''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="2" | 0x39
(57)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Action
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>2</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The ID of the action
*Block list selected = 0
*Block list toggled = 1
*Level saved = 2
*Texture pack changed = 5,
*Texture prompt responded = 6
*Third person changed = 7
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | Value
| class="col2 centeralign" | short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>34</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | A return value. For example, a player selects ID '34' from BlockListSelected, should return 34.
|- class="row3"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 5 bytes
|}

<h4>NotifyPositionAction packet</h4>

''Client to server''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="4" | 0x3A
(58)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Action
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>2</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The ID of the action
*Respawned = 3
*Spawn updated = 4
|- class="row2"
| class="col1 centeralign" | x
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>30</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The X coordinate of the event.
|- class="row3"
| class="col1 centeralign" | y
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>68</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The Y coordinate of the event.
|- class="row4"
| class="col1 centeralign" | z
| class="col2 centeralign" | unsigned short
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>87</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | The Z coordinate of the event.
|- class="row5"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 9 bytes
|}

===ToggleBlockList===
Allows servers to open or close the block list menu for the player.

'''Motivation''': Enables custom block list GUI control, letting servers open or close the block list GUI when needed.

'''Client Behavior''': Must open or close the block list menu depending on the value of the toggle field.

'''Server Behavior''': Servers may send this packet to control the visibility of the block list GUI.


<h4>ToggleBlockList packet</h4>
''Server to client''

{| class="wikitable"
|- class="row0"
! class="col0" | Packet ID
! class="col1" | Field Name
! class="col2" | Field Type
! class="col3" | Example
! class="col4" | Notes
|- class="row1"
| class="col0 centeralign" rowspan="1" | 0x3B<br>(59)
| class="col1 centeralign" | Toggle
| class="col2 centeralign" | byte
| class="col3 centeralign" | <code>1</code>
| class="col4 centeralign" | Controls block list visibility.
* 0 = open
* 1 = close
|- class="row2"
! class="col0" | Total Size:
| class="col1 rightalign" colspan="4" | 2 bytes
|}

[[Category:Java Edition Classic protocol]]
